title: AI power demand: Utilities cashing in on data center expansion
content:
[Electrical Power Lines]
LPETTET

INVESTORS SHOULD KNOW: AI data center expansion is creating a surge in power demand. Utilities and independent power producers are building long-term revenue visibility through large-scale electricity supply agreements with technology customers.

BACKGROUND

The rapid buildout of AI infrastructure requires enormous amounts of electricity. Data centers running large language models and GPU clusters consume power at a scale that strains existing grid capacity, pushing technology companies to secure dedicated power supply deals directly with utilities and power producers.

This dynamic has turned power supply into one of the most discussed bottlenecks in the AI buildout. Utilities that can sign long-term load agreements with data center operators gain multi-year revenue visibility. Independent power producers with flexible generation assets, including natural gas, nuclear, and renewables, are also positioning to serve this demand.

STOCKS TO WATCH

Key publicly traded companies in this space include Bloom Energy Corporation (BE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BE]), Talen Energy Corporation (TLN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TLN]), Oklo Inc. (OKLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKLO]), and The AES Corporation (AES [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AES]) — each classified as AI power and energy suppliers. American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AEP]) is a major regulated utility with direct exposure to large-load data center agreements. TransAlta Corporation (TAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TAC]) and Power Solutions International, Inc. (PSIX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSIX]) also operate in the AI power supply ecosystem. Fermi Inc. (FRMI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FRMI]) and Capstone Energy+, Inc. (CGEH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CGEH]) round out the publicly traded names in this theme.

On the ETF side, the Defiance AI & Power Infrastructure ETF (AIPO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AIPO]) and the Global X Data Center & Digital Infrastructure ETF (DTCR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DTCR]) offer thematic exposure. The Virtus Reaves Utilities ETF (UTES [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UTES]) appears in both the AI power theme and the broader utility ETF universe.

KEY TAKEAWAYS

 	* AI data center growth is driving a measurable increase in electricity demand, creating new revenue opportunities for utilities and power producers willing to sign large-load supply agreements.
 	* Executed multi-year contracts with data center operators are becoming a key indicator of forward revenue visibility for power companies in this space.
 	* Both individual stocks and ETFs focused on utilities and electrification infrastructure offer exposure to this theme, spanning regulated utilities, independent power producers, and thematic funds.

NEWS ROUNDUP

Recent developments show utilities actively converting AI-driven power demand into concrete, long-term supply commitments. The examples below illustrate how contracted load agreements are translating into measurable capacity additions and revenue ramps for power companies.

 	* ALLIANT ENERGY — The company has disclosed five fully executed data center agreements totaling approximately 3.4 GW [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4584289-alliant-outlines-7-percent-earnings-cagr-for-2027minus-2029-as-contracted-data-center-demand], including a new 370 MW electric service agreement with a ramp targeted by the end of 2030. Management cited confidentiality constraints on certain economics, but the agreements represent staged capacity additions and multi-year planning commitments tied directly to data center load growth.
 	* POWERBANK CORPORATION — The company signed a modular data center joint development agreement with Nodiac.ai [https://seekingalpha.com/pr/20566340-modular-data-center-joint-development-agreement-signed-between-powerbank-corporation-and], pairing PowerBank's solar and battery storage systems with Nodiac's co-location model. The approach targets behind-the-meter power delivery at existing generation sites, bypassing lengthy grid interconnection timelines to bring power directly to digital infrastructure.

AI POWER SUPPLIERS IN FOCUS

The screener below identifies stocks classified as AI power and energy suppliers, ranked by relevance to the AI bottlenecks: power and energy theme, with the most theme-aligned companies appearing first.

title: Stifel Maintains Buy Rating on Fermi (FRMI)
content:
Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) is one of the 10 Best New Stocks to Buy Other Than SpaceX.

On June 23, 2026, Stifel lowered its price target on Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) to $17 from $29 and kept a Buy rating. Stifel said Fermi has apparently made progress in the background after months of post-IPO uncertainty, but the continued wait for a firm contract with an initial tenant has "clearly delayed the story and rattled our valuation." The firm updated its forecasts and said Fermi remains "a high-risk, high-reward, pre-revenue story."

On June 11, Citizens said The Information's report that OpenAI is in talks to lease a 10-gigawatt data center in Ohio through funding provided by Nvidia bodes well for Fermi. Citizens said the report improves the likelihood of a significant signed lease for Fermi as the company seeks its first lease for its developing 11 GW natural gas-powered complex. Fermi shares were up 13% to $6.35 following Citizens' note.Stifel Maintains Buy Rating on Fermi (FRMI)

Pixabay/Public Domain

Last month, Evercore ISI analyst Nicholas Amicucci downgraded Fermi to In Line from Outperform with a price target of $11, down from $20. Amicucci said the downgrade is "not a rejection" of the long-term scarcity value of Project Matador or the broader power-demand thesis, but reflects a changed underwriting standard. Amicucci said Fermi 2.0 is "credible evidence" that a reset has begun, but not yet proof that it has worked.

Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) develops next-gen private electric grids that deliver highly redundant gigawatt-scale power to support next-gen intelligence and AI computing.

While we acknowledge the potential of FRMI as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Fermi Selects TSK to Advance Engineering for Texas Power Project
content:
Fermi America has selected Spanish engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC) contractor TSK to provide early works engineering and project management services for three Siemens SGT6-5000F gas turbines that will form the backbone of the second phase of its Project Matador development in Texas.

Under the agreement, TSK will carry out engineering and management work needed to advance permitting, support site mobilization, and oversee the Siemens contract-related engineering scope. The company said the work is intended to shorten development timelines, reduce execution risk, and keep the project on track for future power generation.

TSK, Spain's largest power-focused EPC contractor, has experience delivering industrial and energy infrastructure projects in more than 50 countries. The company specializes in turnkey power generation, electrical infrastructure, and industrial facilities.

Fermi executives said the agreement marks another step in advancing Project Matador, with Vice President of Construction Chad Ingersoll highlighting TSK's role in accelerating permitting and coordinating engineering activities alongside Siemens.

Project Matador is Fermi America's flagship behind-the-meter power campus designed to supply electricity directly to hyperscale artificial intelligence and advanced computing facilities. The company plans to develop a private energy system capable of producing up to 17 GW through a combination of combined-cycle natural gas generation, nuclear power, grid electricity, solar generation and battery energy storage.

The three Siemens SGT6-5000F gas turbines are expected to anchor the project's second development phase, providing dispatchable generation while longer-term energy infrastructure is built out. The announcement reflects the broader trend of technology and infrastructure developers investing in dedicated power assets as surging AI-driven electricity demand reshapes North American energy markets.

By Charles Kennedy for Oilprice.com

title: Fermi (FRMI) Is Down 7.4% After Governance Rift Deepens Around Project Matador Strategy Shift
content:
In June 2026, Fermi Inc. and co-founder and major shareholder Toby R. Neugebauer engaged in an escalating governance dispute over his consent solicitation to call a special shareholder meeting and influence the Company's direction and Project Matador. Amid this contest, Fermi advanced Project Matador by signing a service agreement with TSK Electronica y Electricidad USA for three Siemens SGT6-5000F gas turbines, aiming to accelerate engineering, permitting, and execution for its next phase of power capacity. With Neugebauer pushing for a special meeting and a dual-track review for Project Matador, we'll examine how this governance battle reshapes Fermi's investment narrative.

Capitalize on the AI infrastructure supercycle with our selection of the 49 best 'picks and shovels' of the AI gold rush converting record-breaking demand into massive cash flow.

Fermi Investment Narrative Recap

To own Fermi today, you need to believe its massive, pre revenue Project Matador can convert permitted power and turbines into long duration tenant leases before funding or execution risks bite. The governance fight between Fermi and co founder Toby Neugebauer raises near term uncertainty around control and capital allocation, but does not yet clearly change the core near term catalyst of securing the first large, investment grade tenant or the key risk of financing and schedule slippage.

The new service agreement with TSK Electronica y Electricidad USA for three Siemens SGT6-5000F turbines ties directly into that catalyst by moving Matador's next phase closer to being construction ready. TSK's early works and engineering support help advance permitting and site preparation, which matters because Fermi is building power ahead of tenant fit out, and any delay or mismatch between generation and tenant MEP build could push out the timing of initial cash flows.

But against this operational progress, investors should also weigh the governance overhang and the risk that concentrated shareholder power could influence...

title: Fermi (FRMI) Climbs 9.9% Further on Lingering OpenAI Buzz
content:
Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) is one of the 10 Stocks Making Investors Richer.

Fermi extended its winning streak to a 6th straight session on Wednesday, jumping 9.90 percent to close at $8.66 apiece, as investors resumed buying positions amid ongoing buzz that OpenAI was planning to lease its Project Matador campus.

Last week, JMP Citizens reported that OpenAI could be among the companies assessing Fermi Inc.'s (NASDAQ:FRMI) capacity at the Project Matador, with the two firms already nearing an agreement.

Rendering of Fermi's 17-GW Project Matador. Photo from Fermi website

Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) earlier said that it was on track to deliver approximately 1.1 gigawatts of new power at its Amarillo campus by the end of the year. The entire project is designed to deliver 17 GW of energy by 2030.

Last month, it also hinted at securing a leasing agreement with a new tenant over the next three months, details of which have not been divulged.

In other news, Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) said that it mailed a consent revocation statement to its shareholders enclosing a white consent revocation card in response to the consent solicitation by former CEO Toby Neugebauer seeking to call a special shareholders meeting.

"Fermi is building on its entrepreneurial foundation and scaling the business to support long-term growth and execution. The Company is seeing real results across the business and achieving major construction, regulatory, and financing milestones. Despite this meaningful momentum, Fermi's former CEO, Toby Neugebauer, is soliciting support to obtain the authority to call a Special Meeting in the hopes of filling the Fermi Board of Directors with his own hand-picked nominees to advance his personal agenda at your expense," it said.

While we acknowledge the potential of FRMI as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Why Fermi Rallied Today
content:
Shares of Fermi (NASDAQ: FRMI) rallied as much as 16.5% on Wednesday, before settling into an 8.7% gain on the day, even as markets broadly sold off.

Fermi has been a controversial but very interesting AI infrastructure stock, given the early stage of its data center campus build-out and vast ambitions.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Fermi is building a massive power-delivery and AI data center campus, called Matador, in West Texas. However, as of today, it has not signed up a customer. Back in April, Fermi's CEO and CFO resigned amid a broad shake-up, in an apparent admission that its current leadership wasn't executing on drawing in customers to the project.

But yesterday, a Wall Street analyst circulated a rumor that the company may be working with artificial intelligence pioneer OpenAI as a Matador customer.

JMP flags OpenAI's open interest

Late in the day yesterday, JMP Securities said that OpenAI has been assessing project Matador for its computing needs going forward. The note sent Fermi's stock soaring late in the trading day yesterday, and today the stock saw a follow-on rally.

In its most recent presentation, Fermi noted that it was on track to deliver 1.5 gigawatts (GW) at its site by 2027, on the way to an ultimate ambition for 17 GW by 2038.

It's unclear exactly how interested OpenAI is or what capacity it seeks. 1.5 GW is a large amount of capacity in and of itself. Investors should bear in mind that 1 GW is roughly equivalent to the output of one nuclear power plant. So, 17 GW would ultimately be a massive, massive project. Additionally, Matador is designed to deliver behind-the-meter power, or direct power, without connecting to the electric grid in Texas. That's another tantalizing plus.Image source: Getty Images.

Big ambitions, big risks

It should be noted that there is no firm agreement between OpenAI and Fermi; yesterday's report was just speculation over "interest" and "talks." Additionally, there are several significant risks associated with Fermi as a stock.

First, this massive build-out will require capital investment before revenues and profits are generated. Second, there appears to be a power struggle within Fermi at the board level.

Former CEO Toby Neugebauer remains on the board and controls an outsize stake in Fermi's shares. Meanwhile, it appears he is still trying to wrest control of the company from the independent board members and install his own choice for CEO, Double Eagle Chairman and CEO John Sellers. However, one month ago, the Board sent a letter to shareholders warning that Neugebauer is trying to take control by calling a special meeting and holding a new election of board members. In the letter, the board also accused Neugebauer of damaging relationships with business partners during his time as CEO, among other conduct that led to his ousting.

Story Continues

Regardless of who is right, it appears there is an ongoing power struggle within the company. Combined with the uncertainty over Matador's build-out and the signing of any definitive client agreements, the stock remains too speculative for long-term investors.

Should you buy stock in Fermi right now?

Before you buy stock in Fermi, consider this:

The Motley Fool Stock Advisor analyst team just identified what they believe are the 10 best stocks for investors to buy now… and Fermi wasn't one of them. The 10 stocks that made the cut could produce monster returns in the coming years.

Consider when Netflix made this list on December 17, 2004... if you invested $1,000 at the time of our recommendation, you'd have $424,531!* Or when Nvidia made this list on April 15, 2005... if you invested $1,000 at the time of our recommendation, you'd have $1,273,016!*

title: Real estate stocks continue to post gains, outperform broader markets
content:
[REIT real estate investment trust symbol. Concept words REIT real estate investment trust on the note on beautiful orange background. Business REIT real estate investment trust concept.]
Dzmitry Dzemidovich/iStock via Getty Images

Real estate stocks continued their winning streak, outperforming broader markets to close the week on a positive note.

The S&P 500 Real Estate Index Sector (SP500-60 [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SP500-60]) increased 1.31% to close at 287.01 points, while the accompanying State Street Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLRE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLRE]) gained 1.48% to $45.36.

The Dow Jones REIT Indx Equity REIT Total Return Index (REIT:IND [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REIT:IND]) rose 1.88%, while the FTSE Nareit All Equity REITs index advanced 1.74%.

Kimco Realty (KIM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KIM]) led the largecap gainers, adding 6.93% W/W to $25.91.

The stock hit a 52-week high this week after pricing [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4602516-kimco-realty-unit-prices-upsized-525m-exchangeable-notes-at-350] an upsized $525M 3.50% exchangeable senior notes offering. The notes saw strong investor demand, prompting Stifel Nicolaus to raise the price target to $28.

BXP (BXP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BXP]) (+6.34% W/W to $66.28), Public Storage (PSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSA]) (+5.25% W/W to $325.94), and KE Holdings (BEKE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BEKE]) (+5.04% W/W to $16.89) followed in the list.

Evercore ISI raised its price target on Public Storage to $317 from $306 to reflect recent sector data and valuation roll-forwards.

American Tower (AMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMT]) (-3.58% W/W to $192.50) and CoStar Group (CSGP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSGP]) (-3.10% W/W to $32.84) led the losers.

CSGP dipped as Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) said [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4602725-google-expanding-home-listings-across-all-50-states-competitors-shares-under-water] it is rolling out an enhanced version of Local Services Ads for Home Listings with real estate listings across all 50 states, linking homebuyers with realtors in one platform. The expansion of Google's move into the real estate market is weighing on shares of competitors.

Among midcap stocks, Fermi (FRMI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FRMI]) led the gainers, rising 23.53% week-over-week to $7.14. FRMI shares jumped after talk surfaced over OpenAI's AI infrastructure plans and drew traders to data-center builders with ready power.

Compass (COMP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COMP]) (+11.41% W/W to $8.59) was the second biggest gainer.

CareTrust REIT (CTRE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CTRE]) was the top loser, retreating 2.95% week-over-week to $36.85.

Chatham Lodging Trust (CLDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLDT]) (+12.41% W/W to $12.86), Transcontinental Realty Investors (TCI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TCI]) (+12.05% W/W to $41.47), Hudson Pacific Properties (HPP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPP]) (+11.28% W/W to $15.29), Hotel101 Global Holdings (HBNB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HBNB]) (-7.51% W/W to $5.24), and Apartment Investment and Management Company (AIV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AIV]) (-4.95% W/W to $2.88) were the smallcap movers.

title: Fermi (FRMI) Powers 22.6% After Earning This AI Giant’s Backing
content:
Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) is one of the 10 Stocks Surviving Market Slaughter.

Fermi Inc. snapped a three-day losing streak on Wednesday, surging 22.60 percent to finish at $6.89 apiece, following news reports that OpenAI was planning to ink a deal from its Project Matador campus.

JMP Citizens reportedly said that OpenAI was among the companies assessing Fermi Inc.'s (NASDAQ:FRMI) capacity at the Project Matador, with the two firms nearing an agreement.

Rendering of Fermi's 17-GW Project Matador. Photo from Fermi website

Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) earlier said that it was on track to deliver approximately 1.1 gigawatts of new power at its Amarillo campus by the end of the year. The entire project is designed to deliver 17 GW of energy by 2030.

Last month, Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) hinted at securing a leasing agreement with a new tenant over the next three months, details of which have not been divulged.

It said that it is also working diligently to hire its next CEO with the help of executive recruiting firm Heidrick & Struggles, following the ouster of Toby Neugebauer two weeks earlier. It is likewise exploring strategic partnerships to accelerate the deployment of power and data centers.

"Fermi America is at a meaningful inflection point in its development," Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) Chairman Marius Haas said.

In other news, Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ:FRMI) markedly widened its net loss in the first quarter of the year to $188.69 million from $78 million in the same period last year, dragged by a higher operating loss of $166 million versus only $78 million year-on-year.

While we acknowledge the potential of FRMI as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on thebest short-term AI stock.

READ NEXT: 33 Stocks That Should Double in 3 Years and Cathie Wood 2026 Portfolio: 10 Best Stocks to Buy.

Disclosure: None. Follow Insider Monkey on Google News.

title: Fermi's Chief Nuclear Officer Sold Nearly 160,000 Company Shares for $1 Million. Here's What That Means for Investors.
content:
Key Points

Mesut Uzman sold 158,541 shares for a total of ~$1.0 million on June 3, 2026, at a weighted average price of around $6.31 per share. Shares were sold from both direct accounts and indirect holdings attributed to Uzman’s spouse, with no derivative securities involved in this filing. After the sale, Uzman retains 1,341,459 common shares (670,968 direct, 670,491 indirect).10 stocks we like better than Fermi ›

Mesut Uzman, Chief Nuclear Construction Officer of Fermi Inc.(NASDAQ:FRMI), disclosed the sale of 158,541 shares across direct and indirect accounts on June 3, 2026, for a transaction value of approximately $1.0 million according to a SEC Form 4 filing.

Transaction summary MetricValueShares sold158,541Shares sold (direct)79,032Shares sold (indirect)79,509Transaction value$1.0 millionPost-transaction shares (direct)670,968Post-transaction shares (indirect)670,491Post-transaction value (direct ownership)~$4.2 million

Transaction and post-transaction values based on SEC Form 4 weighted average reported price ($6.31).

Key questions

How does this sale impact Uzman’s total exposure to Fermi Inc.?
After the transaction, Uzman maintains 670,968 common shares directly and 670,491 common shares indirectly.What portion of Uzman’s aggregate holdings was sold, and how does this compare to direct and indirect ownership buckets?
The sale involved 10.57% of total pre-transaction holdings, split nearly evenly between direct and indirect accounts, reflecting a balanced approach to liquidity without disproportionate impact on either ownership type.Were any derivative securities or options exercised or transacted as part of this filing?
This transaction involved only sales of common stock from direct and indirect holdings; there were no derivative securities or option exercises disclosed in this Form 4.Does this transaction represent a change in selling cadence or a response to reduced available shares?
As Uzman’s first open-market sale and given a remaining holdings ratio of 0.447, the transaction’s scale is explained by available share capacity rather than a change in trading pattern.

Company overview MetricValuePrice (as of market close 6/3/26)$6.09Market capitalization$3.69 billionNet income (TTM)($718.38 million)

Company snapshot

Fermi develops energy infrastructure and data center campuses designed to support artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure growth.It operates as a regulated electric utility, focusing on building and managing large-scale infrastructure assets.The company serves enterprise customers seeking scalable, reliable energy and compute solutions for next-generation AI workloads.

Fermi Inc. develops energy and data center infrastructure to support the needs of to-be-built AI infrastructure, and operates as a regulated electric utility.

What this transaction means for investors

The June 3 sale of Fermi stock by Mesut Uzman comes at a time when shares had plunged, having reached a 52-week low of $4.47 on April 9. This is a huge drop from the $21 per share price announced for its IPO in the fall of 2025.

Consequently, Uzman’s sale can appear worrisome to investors, especially in light of the chaos Fermi is experiencing with the ouster of its CEO Toby Neugebauer and his calls to sell the company. However, Uzman’s transaction was executed to cover tax obligations related to the vesting of company shares.

In addition, his remaining holdings include 500,000 shares of restricted stock units that have yet to vest, meaning he cannot sell these at this time. Given these factors, Uzman’s disposition is not a cause for investor concern.

The larger issue is whether the Rick Perry-founded Fermi can begin to produce revenue. It exited the first quarter with a net loss of $188.7 million. It is still constructing its first AI-focused energy facility, although the company states the project is progressing on schedule.

Should you buy stock in Fermi right now?

Before you buy stock in Fermi, consider this:

The Motley Fool Stock Advisor analyst team just identified what they believe are the 10 best stocks for investors to buy now… and Fermi wasn’t one of them. The 10 stocks that made the cut could produce monster returns in the coming years.

Consider when Netflix made this list on December 17, 2004... if you invested $1,000 at the time of our recommendation, you’d have $443,191!* Or when Nvidia made this list on April 15, 2005... if you invested $1,000 at the time of our recommendation, you’d have $1,258,838!*

Now, it’s worth noting Stock Advisor’s total average return is 941% — a market-crushing outperformance compared to 206% for the S&P 500. Don't miss the latest top 10 list, available with Stock Advisor, and join an investing community built by individual investors for individual investors.
